feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: My Sister-in-Law Is Going to Permanently Disable Her Child. She Won’t Heed My Warnings.
entry.summary: I'm so worried.

entry.title: Cancer cases worldwide are expected to soar in the coming decades, a report finds. Here’s why.
entry.summary: The World Health Organization tempered optimism about improvements in cancer treatment and said global health care inequities are driving more cases and deaths.

entry.title: 'Serious illness': Ohio warns of cyclosporiasis outbreak causing explosive diarrhea
entry.summary: Ohio health officials are urging residents to take extra precautions when handling fruits and vegetables to help prevent a disease that can lead to watery, and sometimes explosive, diarrhea.

entry.title: Healthy Returns: Employers aren't expanding coverage of GLP-1 obesity drugs – many are finding ways around it
entry.summary: Around 36% of employers said they provide coverage of GLP-1s for both diabetes and weight loss, the same as 2025 and up from 34% in 2024, the survey said.

entry.title: The ‘Big Sign’ of Melanoma and Skin Cancer Symptoms a Dermatologist Monitors in Herself
entry.summary: A dermatologist reveals the ‘big sign’ a mole may be melanoma and the skin cancer warning signs she looks for in her own body.

entry.title: Worried About High Cortisol? The No. 1 Thing You Should Do, According to a Doctor
entry.summary: From cortisol cocktails to cortisol face, the “stress hormone” has a bit of a branding problem, says Dr.

entry.title: Scientists Say This 1 Drink May Affect How Your Medication Works
entry.summary: A new study finds that alkaline drinks like mineral water may affect how certain medications act in the body. Here, experts explain why.

entry.title: Doctors Say This 'Embarrassing' Symptom Could Be A Warning Sign Of Colorectal Cancer
entry.summary: It can indicate a more advanced stage of the disease or can be a sign of a different medical condition.

entry.title: One walking habit could signal a healthier brain after 80, scientists say
entry.summary: Super movers, who walk at speeds comparable to people 30 years younger, showed about half the risk of cognitive decline, a Stony Brook Medicine study found.

entry.title: The 7 Best Berries for Your Health, Ranked by Dietitians
entry.summary: Fresh or frozen, berries are packed with antioxidants and fiber. These are the ones dietitians recommend most, from blueberries and blackberries to underrated picks like goldenberries.

entry.title: Do your hormones help build muscle? The answer may surprise you.
entry.summary: A study has upended common beliefs, with implications for women and men of all ages.

entry.title: Diagnostic dilemma: A woman heard voices for years — but not because of psychosis
entry.summary: After multiple hospital visits and unsuccessful antipsychotic treatments, a woman's doctors uncovered an overlooked explanation for her hallucinations.

entry.title: The Best Morning Drink for Supporting Metabolism, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: A balanced smoothie with fiber, protein and healthy fats is one of the best drinks you can sip in the morning to support metabolism. Dietitians share why it’s a good choice.

entry.title: 3 cyclosporiasis cases confirmed in New Mexico, health officials urge hand washing
entry.summary: New Mexico health officials say the state has three confirmed cyclosporiasis cases this year and they urge people to prevent it with hand washing.

entry.title: Creatine doesn't just build muscle. It may also help fight cancer
entry.summary: Scientists have discovered that creatine may strengthen one of the immune system's most important cancer-fighting pathways by energizing dendritic cells that activate killer T cells. The promising results could eventually help make immunotherapy more eff…

entry.title: A woman who has autoimmune gastritis, the same condition as Bryan Johnson, shares what it's like to live with it
entry.summary: Bryan Johnson was diagnosed with autoimmune gastritis, which destroys cells that produce stomach acid. Here's what it's like to live with it.

entry.title: Some health workers in Congo's Ebola outbreak go on strike over pay issues as deaths near 600
entry.summary: Healthcare workers at the epicenter of Congo’s Ebola outbreak are walking off their jobs to protest delays in their payments. The strike threatens efforts to slow the outbreak that officials say continues to spread faster than the response. The latest governm…

entry.title: Brain tumors: New vaccine offers patients hope for more time
entry.summary: A new therapy offers people with seemingly incurable brain tumors scope for hope: It could slow the progression of the disease and extend patients' lives.

entry.title: Painless Swab Detects Oral Cancers in an Hour, With Over 95% Accuracy
entry.summary: Oral cancer might not be one that many people think to look out for, but its prevalence is rising and it can be deadly.

entry.title: Measles outbreak confirmed at Arizona immigration detention center
entry.summary: Federal officials confirm seven active cases of measles at an Arizona immigration detention center, prompting quarantines and restrictions on visitation.

entry.title: First locally acquired Chagas infection confirmed in San Diego County
entry.summary: On Monday, San Diego County health officials announced that they had confirmed the first locally acquired Chagas infection in the region.

entry.title: ‘Severe outbreak’ of contagious cancer detected in Washington soft-shell clams
entry.summary: Although it is possible for the disease to spread to Oregon, an ODFW spokesperson told KOIN 6 there is “currently a low risk.”

entry.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreaks: What to know about the foodborne illness and symptoms
entry.summary: The source of cyclosporiasis outbreaks is still unknown but fresh produce is suspected.

entry.title: Scientists Think They've Finally Identified How Alzheimer's Kills Brain Cells
entry.summary: To help cure Alzheimer's disease and other types of dementia, we need to understand the mechanisms through which it damages the brain – including how the toxic build-up of proteins inside neurons eventually causes their death.

entry.title: A PharmD Shares 4 Supplements That May Benefit Older Adults
entry.summary: Best supplements for older adults include vitamin D, B12, calcium, and omega-3s. Learn who may benefit and when to talk with your provider.

entry.title: The Best Late-Night Habit to Help You Poop in the A.M.
entry.summary: Sticking to a consistent sleep schedule is the best late-night habit to support morning bowel movements.

entry.title: Inflammation Forces Brain Stem Cells to Halt Neurogenesis
entry.summary: A new study reveals how chronic inflammation halts adult neurogenesis by altering hippocampal stem cells.

entry.title: Deadly Legionnaires' disease outbreak sparks concern in major US city: Know the symptoms
entry.summary: A Legionnaires' disease outbreak on Manhattan's Upper East Side has caused 23 confirmed cases and 17 hospitalizations linked to cooling towers.

entry.title: Sex advice: I heard something ghastly can happen in bed to women like me. I need to change that.
entry.summary: I want to avoid this fate.

entry.title: Psychology says boomers who feel invisible in their 60s and 70s aren't imagining it — research consistently finds that Western cultures systematically withdraw social attention from older adults in ways that measurably alter how those adults see themselves
entry.summary: I first watched my mother disappear at a dinner party about three years ago. Nobody was rude. That is the strange part. She was sitting right there, glass in

entry.title: What to know about Legionnaires' disease making people sick on New York's Upper East Side
entry.summary: A Legionnaires’ disease cluster in New York City has sickened more than 20 people in two Upper East Side neighborhoods. The disease is a type of pneumonia caused by Legionella bacteria. They grow in warm water and can spread in building water systems such as …

entry.title: Early sexual initiation accelerates physical aging, large genetic study finds
entry.summary: A genetic study reveals that having sex at an earlier age may accelerate the physical aging process, creating a lifelong ripple effect that increases frailty and reduces overall longevity.

entry.title: The ADHD Diagnosis Women Don’t Get Until Midlife Breaks Them
entry.summary: Wondering if it’s ADHD, perimenopause brain fog or both? Learn symptom clues, timing differences, and what to track before seeking help.

entry.title: I’m a doctor — the 4 symptoms I wish men would stop hiding
entry.summary: Between 2015 and 2022, men were more likely to be diagnosed with 16 different late-stage cancers than women, according to a new study.

entry.title: Tattoo ink doesn’t just stay in the skin — a PNAS mouse study found pigments can reach nearby lymph nodes soon after contact, damage macrophages, and keep inflammation active for at least two months
entry.summary: A group of researchers at the Università della Svizzera italiana gave mice small foot tattoos in black, red or green, then watched under the microscope where

entry.title: Case of deadly ‘kissing bug’ found in California — and sufferers may not even know they have it
entry.summary: The blood-sucking "kissing bug" has officially spread its fatal disease in SoCal.

entry.title: The Hectic Modern World Is Shredding Your Little Cave Man Brain, Scientists Confirm
entry.summary: Our brains evolved to be hyper-aware of what happened in the small communities we originally grew up in, and it's now backfiring.

entry.title: Not all birth controls are equal, some are linked to higher risk of brain tumors, study finds
entry.summary: Meningiomas are the most common brain tumors in adults, accounting for 38% to 42% of all primary central nervous system tumors. According to 2021 WHO data, 874 million of the world's 1.9 billion women of reproductive age use modern contraceptive methods. Many…

entry.title: What to know about cyclospora, the gut-churning parasite causing illness in several states, and how to avoid it
entry.summary: Got a nasty stomach bug that won’t go away? It could be cyclosporiasis, a parasitic infection that causes weeks of debilitating diarrhea, cramps and bloating.

entry.title: Are You Getting The Wrong Cholesterol Test? Study Says Your Standard Test May Miss Heart Attack Risks
entry.summary: Millions of people get their cholesterol levels tested every year to track their heart health. Most of these tests measure low-density lipoprotein (LDL) choles

entry.title: How to avoid dementia — what the science really says
entry.summary: Ambitious studies have been examining the protective effects of diet, exercise and socializing — with surprising results.

entry.title: Dear Doctor: Are drug companies suppressing peptide use because it’s a better, cheaper cure?
entry.summary: Peptides that people buy at health food stores or online aren't thoroughly tested.

entry.title: Bellevue teen builds app to monitor Parkinson’s, Alzheimer’s and ALS
entry.summary: Ilina Rao, 16, of Bellevue has spent the past year building NeuroSpeak, an app which uses AI to help users screen for Parkinson's disease, Alzheimer's and ALS.

entry.title: Largest study yet reveals which cancers have their own microbiomes
entry.summary: Knowing which cancer tumours have their own microbiomes could lead to more personalised treatments.

entry.title: There's no treatment designed for the Ebola strain ravaging DRC. But now there's hope
entry.summary: The strain of the virus that's responsible for the current outbreak has no specific treatments or preventive measures. Three new clinical trials could provide possible breakthroughs.

entry.title: What to know about the safety of this popular supplement for arthritis
entry.summary: Many people use glucosamine, though evidence of its effectiveness is mixed at best, experts said.

entry.title: How strength training can help women keep a healthy heart
entry.summary: Mobility coach Dana Santas shows how integrating resistance training into a weekly fitness regimen can benefit women’s heart health.

entry.title: A Common Joint Supplement Is Now Tied to Faster Dementia Progression — Are You at Risk?
entry.summary: Millions take this supplement for achy joints. New research says it may carry a hidden risk for some.

entry.title: Different forms of intelligence show unique genetic links to psychiatric conditions
entry.summary: A new genetic analysis reveals that the DNA linked to conditions like schizophrenia and ADHD has specific, diverging effects on different types of intelligence, separating how we process information from what we know.

entry.title: Common gym supplement could help fight depression, new research suggests
entry.summary: A systematic review found creatine monohydrate may help as an add-on treatment for major depressive disorder, but researchers say the evidence is mixed.
